channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California officials reopen certain Palisades Fire zones, cops to use planes to monitor looting
item.description: California officials will reopen some Palisades Fire evacuation zones, as law enforcement ramps up security to address looting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A$AP Rocky turns down plea deal as trial opens on charges he fired a gun at a former friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A$AP Rocky turned down a final prosecution plea offer of 180 days in jail, risking the possibility of a guilty verdict and years in prison as jury selection began at his trial on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Elephants can't pursue their release from a Colorado zoo because they're not human, court says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Five elephants at a Colorado zoo may be “majestic” but, since they're not human, they do not have the legal right to pursue their release, Colorado’s highest court said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Warnings for dangerous winds extended in Southern California as new wildfires break out
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Forecasters warned that dangerous winds will buffet Southern California for at least two more days as new wildfires broke out on Tuesday, a pair of major Los Angeles-area blazes burned for a third week, and officials made preparations to protect scorched neighborhoods from toxic a
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KREX 5

item.title: Fire chief gunned down while helping stranded driver
item.description: CHAMBERS COUNTY, Ala. (WRBL) – A Coweta County battalion fire chief was killed Sunday while stopping to help a stranded driver on County Road 267 in Chambers County, Alabama, according to the Chambers County Sheriff’s Office. The incident occurred shortly before 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California fires: Friars Fire breaks out near Fashion Valley Mall in San Diego
item.description: A mandatory evacuation order was issued Tuesday afternoon after a new brush fire, named the "Friars Fire," broke out near Fashion Valley Mall in San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump wants to make showerheads and toilets flow greatly again, but so may utility bills
item.description: On his first day in office, President Donald Trump announced plans to pull America from the Paris Agreement and expand the production of oil, both campaign promises.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John And Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Illinois among 22 states suing to stop Trump's order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: CHICAGO — Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul on Tuesday joined three other states in a lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump's executive order that seeks to end birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, KTLA 5

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court declines to step into former GOP Rep. Steve King's meme fight
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday declined to revive former Rep. Steve King’s (R-Iowa) bid to receive attorneys’ fees in a copyright fight that stemmed from a meme posted by his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump goes toe-to-toe with sanctuary cities over deportation as illegal immigration crackdown set to begin
item.description: Sanctuary cities, like Chicago and San Diego, have doubled down on their commitment to be a safe haven for migrants and refuse to assist in federal deportation efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bay Area tech CEOs get front-row seats at Trump inauguration
item.description: (KRON) -- The biggest tech leaders of Silicon Valley got a front-row seat at President Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday. There has been a sudden support this administration has gained from the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Sara Stinson, KRON4

item.title: 250 roosters, guns, cash seized in Southern California cockfighting bust
item.description: (KTLA) - Authorities in San Bernardino County arrested two men, detained 33 others and seized more than 200 fighting roosters, U.S. currency and dozens of firearms, officials announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio State wins 1st national title since 2014, outlasting Notre Dame 34-23 in CFP championship game
item.description: Instead of crying over another collapse, Ohio State can celebrate another national title after holding off a Notre Dame comeback bid Monday night to walk away with a nailbiter of a 34-23 victory over the Fighting Irish.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Fire crews in Southern California quickly extinguish brush fires amid extreme fire weather
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters quickly extinguished several brush fires that erupted Monday in Southern California amid windy and dry conditions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, ABC 4

item.title: Do US adults support Trump's Day 1 actions? Here's what polling shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's second term began with efforts to deliver on key campaign promises, including his vows to crack down on immigration and restore “ energy dominance.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Mt. Denali to be renamed Mt. McKinley, Trump says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — President Donald Trump announced that he would be renaming Alaska's Mt. Denali, the highest peak in North America, as part of his first-day actions in office during his inauguration. "We will restore the name of a great president, William McKinley, to Mt.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted Sunday to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5
